evidence: Docstring: 'If f(z) = ∑ aₖzⁿₖ is an entire function ... is it true that f assumes every value infinitely often?'
verify_note: decl erdos_517 exists (research open, AMS 30); QID live, label exact; entire functions are the objects of the statement

evidence: The hypothesis is HasFabryGaps n (nₖ/k → ∞), the gap condition of the Fabry gap theorem.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; hypothesis HasFabryGaps (n_k/k -> inf) is exactly the Fabry gap condition, load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: entire function with Fejér gaps (∑ 1/nₖ < ∞) assumes every value infinitely often, proved in [Bi28].
verify_note: decl erdos_517.variants.fejer exists (research solved); QID live; statement is about entire functions

evidence: The statement concerns gap (lacunary) power series f(z) = ∑ aₖ zⁿₖ with sparse exponents.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; gap power series with sparse exponents = lacunary function, load-bearing

evidence: References cite Biró, 'On a problem of Turán concerning sums of powers of complex numbers' and 'Turán's pure power sum problem'; the statement bounds max_k |∑ zᵢᵏ|.
verify_note: decl erdos_519 exists (research solved, AMS 30); QID live; the statement is Turan's pure power sum problem, the subject of Turan's method

evidence: The statement max(|A+A|,|AA|) ≫ |A|^(2-ε) is the Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product conjecture (erdosproblems.com tags it 'sum-product problem').
verify_note: decl erdos_52 exists (research open, AMS 11); QID live; statement is the Erdos-Szemeredi sum-product conjecture, covered by that item/article; mentions is the right kind

evidence: The statement bounds the cardinality of the sumset A+A (and product set AA) of a finite set of integers.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; A+A sumset cardinality is the core quantity

evidence: The statement is about random Rademacher multiplicative functions f with f(ab)=f(a)f(b) for coprime a,b.
verify_note: decl erdos_520 exists (research open, AMS 11 60); QID live; IsRademacherMultiplicative requires f(ab)=f(a)f(b) for coprime a,b — multiplicative function is load-bearing

evidence: Asks for an almost-sure limsup of ∑_{m≤N} f(m) / sqrt(N log log N), a law-of-the-iterated-logarithm normalization.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; sqrt(N log log N) limsup normalization is precisely a LIL analogue

evidence: Docstring: monochromatic finite subset sums for a 2-colouring of N, 'Proved by Hindman [Hi74] (for any number of colours)' — this is Hindman's finite sums theorem.
verify_note: decl erdos_532 exists (research solved, AMS 5); QID live, label 'Hindman's theorem' exact; statement = monochromatic finite subset sums for a colouring of N — this IS Hindman's finite sums theorem (2-colour form, equivalent by standard iteration; docstring credits Hindman [Hi74])

evidence: Docstring defines the triangle Ramsey–Turán number RT₃(n, K₅, m) and asks whether δ₃(5) = 0, citing the Turán-Ramsey theorems of [EHSSS94].
verify_note: decl erdos_533 exists (research solved, AMS 5); QID live; the problem defines and asks about the triangle Ramsey-Turan number RT_3(n,K_5,m)

evidence: Asks whether a dense K₅-free graph must contain a set of ≫ n vertices spanning no triangle (a triangle-free vertex set).
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; 'set of vertices spanning no triangle' (CliqueFreeOn ... 3) is the triangle-free condition, load-bearing

evidence: f_r(N) is the largest subset of {1,...,N} with no r-element subset having the same pairwise greatest common divisor.
verify_note: decl erdos_535 exists (research open, AMS 5 11); QID live; constant pairwise gcd is the defining condition of f_r(N)

evidence: Docstring: Abbott pointed out the ordinary sunflower conjecture does not suffice; this is the stronger sunflower-type auxiliary conjecture with Ω(n)=k.
verify_note: decl erdos_535.variants.sunflower_strong exists (research open, AMS 5 11); QID live; docstring is explicitly the stronger sunflower-type conjecture (Abbott/Erdos-Rado)

evidence: Docstring: must there be distinct a,b,c in A with [a,b]=[b,c]=[a,c], 'where [·,·] denotes the least common multiple'.
verify_note: decl erdos_536 exists (research open, AMS 11); QID live; the equalities [a,b]=[b,c]=[a,c] are lcm equalities, load-bearing
